subject: A large spider viewed from directly above, centered in a web amid dried autumn leaves
description: The painting shows a large spider from an overhead perspective, centered in the composition. The spider's body fills much of the frame — it has multiple visible eyes on a rounded, textured head, and eight legs spread outward in a radial pattern. Fine white web strands cross over the spider and connect to the surrounding dried leaves and plant material. The leaves are brown and amber with crinkled, autumnal textures. The entire color palette is warm brown and tan with white web highlights. The mood is unsettling in its intimacy — the viewer is positioned directly above the spider as if looking down into its domain.
foreground: Large spider centered in web among dried leaves
background: Dried autumn leaves and web strands
emotion: still, patient, predatory — the spider waits
